column1: # grep-tells.tsv — TIER 1 (flat-presence) rules for audit-swiftui-navigation-toolbars (nav-01 … nav-12).
# severity: hard | warn | adv   (hard → runner exits 2). The runner greps each ERE over the target *.swift.
# This LOCATES only — the LLM agent READS each hit (SKILL.md step 3) and decides. The one structural rule
# grep cannot express (a 3-col NavigationSplitView whose content: closure is just EmptyView() — nav-03)
# lives in ast-grep/*.yml (tier 2). nav-04 (boolean+frame column-hide) and nav-08 (columnWidth INSIDE the
# detail: closure) are kept here as grep tells, READ-confirmed, because neither makes a clean ast-grep rule
# without heavy false positives (see ast-grep/nav-03-empty-middle-column.yml NOTE). Full ❌→✅ + sources:

column4: topBar* placement is UNAVAILABLE on macOS (compile error) → semantic .navigation/.primaryAction (nav-05, fix_mode: auto)
navigationBar* placement is deprecated iOS-only → .navigation/.primaryAction (nav-06, fix_mode: auto)
iOS navigation-bar title concept — no-op on macOS → .navigationTitle/.navigationSubtitle (nav-07, fix_mode: auto)
ToolbarSpacer/SpacerSizing is macOS 26.0+ — REPORT only if deployment target < macOS 26 (read in ORIENT); else verify it is gated #available(macOS 26, *) (nav-11)
NavigationView is DEPRECATED (through macOS 26.5) → NavigationSplitView (sidebar+detail) / NavigationStack (drill-down). cross_ref api-currency (nav-01)
NavigationStack — if it is the TOP-LEVEL shell wrapping a sidebar List(selection:), it is the wrong Mac shell → NavigationSplitView; fine for drill-down inside a column. READ to confirm role (nav-02)
NavigationSplitView — if a 3-col init(sidebar:content:detail:) has an EmptyView()/placeholder content:, use the 2-col init(sidebar:detail:). READ the closures (nav-03)
a .frame(...width: 0) — if it collapses a column driven by a boolean instead of a columnVisibility binding, that is a frame hack (nav-04). READ for the NavigationSplitView context

navigationSplitViewColumnWidth — SILENT no-op on the DETAIL column; constrains leading columns only. For a resizable inspector use HSplitView/.inspector. UNVERIFIED — verify against Xcode 26 SDK (nav-08)
a sidebar List(selection:) — if it is the NavigationSplitView sidebar and lacks .listStyle(.sidebar), the material/selection highlight is wrong on macOS (nav-09)
.searchable — put it on the NavigationSplitView, not on a column, or it lands in the wrong toolbar slot on macOS (nav-10)
detail: closure — an empty/blank detail should be ContentUnavailableView, the native empty-state component (nav-12). READ the closure body
